mnemonics.title: OS Services Main Categories
mnemonics.content: UI, Program Exec, I/O, File System, Communication, Error Detection, Resource Allocation, Protection, Accounting. Remember: 'U-P-I-F-C-E-R-P-A' or think of it as USER-FOCUSED services: User Interface, File management, Execution, Communication, Error handling, Resource management, Protection, Accounting.

mnemonics.title: Remember UI Types
mnemonics.content: CLI = Command Line Interface (text), GUI = Graphical User Interface (visuals), Batch = command scripts. Mnemonic: 'CGB - Can Go Batch' for Command, Graphical, Batch.

acronyms.usage: Text-based interaction where users type commands
Visual-based interaction using windows, icons, menus
Data structure storing process information maintained by OS
Mechanism for processes to communicate and synchronize
Set of functions applications use to request OS services

visualTips.visualization: Draw a triangle: Top = Users, Middle = OS, Bottom = Hardware. Arrows show OS managing both directions.
Layer diagram: Hardware at bottom, OS kernel in middle, System Calls above, Applications at top. Services flow downward.
Pie chart showing CPU, Memory, I/O divided among processes - OS manages this division.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant manager who takes orders (user requests), coordinates kitchen (hardware), and serves food (services) to customers.
Reception (UI), Room allocation (Memory), Housekeeping (I/O), Security (Protection), Billing (Accounting) - all work together.
Librarian organizes books (files), maintains catalog (directory), manages borrowing (file operations) - just like OS file service.
